__fixture__.name: restricted_reopen_managed_fallback
__fixture__.scenario: Managed policy, a wrong-target detection, a trust-state drift, or a helper attach failure forced a restricted reopen. The user can still inspect local state, but remote writes and managed control-plane mutations are suspended until reapproval lands. There is no local reversal; widening requires a reviewed reapproval flow or an escalation packet.

title: Restricted reopen as managed fallback
summary: Restricted reopen suspends remote writes, managed control-plane mutations, and trust widening while preserving authored state, trust posture, credentials, and helper authorization. There is no local reversal path; widening requires an explicit reapproval or an escalation packet.
preconditions: Supervisor or Doctor observed route_recovery_required, policy_blocked, or manual_review_required on remote_connectors, policy_entitlement_verifier_helpers, or the managed control plane.
Approval ticket, workspace trust posture, or target identity is not in a state the user may widen from without explicit review.

linkage_bindings.notes: current_rung_class = restricted_reopen when a bundle is produced from this rung; redaction defaults to support_bundle_by_reference.
Because the rung has no local reversal, an object-issue handoff packet is the canonical next step; it carries ticket ids, bundle refs, and the Doctor finding code by reference.
Doctor's wrong-target, trust-state, and helper-attach findings are the default triggers for this rung.
Known-limit refs like known_limit:remote_connector.attach_requires_reauth_after_disconnect explain why no local reversal exists.

explanation_fields.preserved_work_summary: Authored files, open buffers, durable indexes, trust posture, credentials, remote helper authorization, managed policy, and any already-exported support bundles all remain untouched.
explanation_fields.disabled_capability_summary: Remote helper attach, managed control-plane writes, workspace trust widening, telemetry upload, and docs-pack live fetch stay disabled until an explicit reapproval lands.
explanation_fields.escalation_summary: Escalate whenever the rung reports no_local_repair_path_available, when the user declines reapproval, when managed policy forces a narrower posture than the user requested, or when an exit attempt produced a denied_by_policy reason.
explanation_fields.user_facing_next_step: Export a recovery-follow-up handoff packet naming the scenario row, the Doctor finding code, and the support-bundle ref. Widen only through the reapproval flow the handoff packet routes to.
